title: Enforce TLS With a Bucket Policy
description:
## Reject Non-HTTPS Requests

A common compliance control is to **deny any S3 request not made over TLS**, using the `aws:SecureTransport` condition.

**Your task:** write a bucket policy at **`/root/bucket-policy.json`** with a statement that **Denies** S3 actions when **`aws:SecureTransport`** is **`false`** (i.e. plain HTTP).

> 💡 Use `"Effect": "Deny"`, `"Action": "s3:*"`, and a `Condition` with `"Bool": { "aws:SecureTransport": "false" }`.

hints.title: Deny-on-insecure-transport
hints.body: A Deny with a Bool condition on aws:SecureTransport=false blocks any non-HTTPS access.

validation.description: Checks for a Deny statement conditioned on aws:SecureTransport=false.

commands.description: Denies requests when SecureTransport is false
commands.command: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('/root/bucket-policy.json')); s=d['Statement']; s=s if isinstance(s,list) else [s]; ok=any(st.get('Effect')=='Deny' and 'securetransport' in json.dumps(st.get('Condition',{})).lower() and 'false' in json.dumps(st.get('Condition',{})).lower() for st in s); print('ok' if ok else 'no')"
